ddls:
CREATE TABLE "brands" (
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brand */,
  "brand_name" TEXT /* Name of the gin brand */,
  "brand_description" TEXT /* Description of the brand */,
  "price_per_750ml" REAL /* Price of the brand per 750-ml bottle */,
  "brand_website" TEXT /* Official website of the brand */,
  "brand_origin" TEXT /* Country of origin for the brand */,
  "brand_established" INTEGER /* Year the brand was established */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "importers" (
  "importer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each importer */,
  "importer_name" TEXT /* Name of the importer */,
  "importer_description" TEXT /* Description of the importer */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the importer */,
  "importer_phone" TEXT /* Phone number for the importer */,
  "importer_address" TEXT /* Physical address of the importer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("importer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "brand_importer_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brand-importer mapping */,
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the brand */,
  "importer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the importer */,
  "contract_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the contract between the brand and importer */,
  "contract_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the contract between the brand and importer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_brand_importer_mapping_brand_id FOREIGN KEY ("brand_id") REFERENCES brands ("brand_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_brand_importer_mapping_importer_id FOREIGN KEY ("importer_id") REFERENCES importers ("importer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_data" (
  "sales_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales record */,
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the brand */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the sales data */,
  "sales_volume" REAL /* Sales volume in thousands of 9-liter case depletions */,
  "percentage_change" REAL /* Year-over-year percentage change in sales volume */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region of the sales data */,
  "distribution_channel" TEXT /* Distribution channel of the sales data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_brand_id FOREIGN KEY ("brand_id") REFERENCES brands ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., sales analyst, marketing manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., sales, marketing, finance) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "sales_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sales record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sales record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sales record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sales_id FOREIGN KEY ("sales_id") REFERENCES sales_data ("sales_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "generated_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, Excel, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
